text.title: reranking-models Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of reranking-models enforces.

rule.statement: Benchmark candidate rerankers on a labeled set against the latency p95 + cost-per-query budgets. Ship only if all targets met.
rule.rationale: Reranker choice without benchmark is guesswork.

rule.statement: When query volume * cost-per-rerank > $X budget, prefer local cross-encoder over API.
rule.rationale: API rerank cost scales linearly with traffic; local amortises.

rule.statement: Local cross-encoder must be warmed at process start (one dummy inference) before serving traffic.
rule.rationale: Cold-start latency on first query is 5-10x steady-state.

rule.statement: Pin the exact reranker version (model name + revision hash) in config; never 'latest'.
rule.rationale: Vendor drift silently changes scores between deploys.

rule.statement: If reranker call fails / times out, fall back to ANN top-K ordering with a degraded flag set; do not crash the request.
rule.rationale: Reranker failure shouldn't take down the whole pipeline.

rule.statement: Skip rerank model selection when single-stage retrieval already meets recall targets.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
